---
title: "Git and Github"
execute:
  eval: false
  echo: true
  warning: false
---

Instruction to set up the git and github repository connection.

## Create a VM SSH key to connect to github

```bash
mkdir -p ~/.ssh
chmod 700 ~/.ssh
ssh-keygen -t ed25519 -C "<your-github-email>" -f ~/.ssh/id_ed25519_bcfms_vm
chmod 600 ~/.ssh/id_ed25519_bcfms_vm
chmod 644 ~/.ssh/id_ed25519_bcfms_vm.pub
```

## Load key and pin it in SSH config

```bash
eval "$(ssh-agent -s)"
ssh-add ~/.ssh/id_ed25519_bcfms_vm
```

```bash
cat >> ~/.ssh/config <<'EOF'
Host github.com
  HostName github.com
  User git
  IdentityFile ~/.ssh/id_ed25519_bcfms_vm
  IdentitiesOnly yes
EOF
chmod 600 ~/.ssh/config
```

## Add key in GitHub

```bash
cat ~/.ssh/id_ed25519_bcfms_vm.pub
```

Copy the full output (public key) and paste it in GitHub. **Do not paste the private key file**.

Choose one:

- Deploy key (read-only) on this repo: clone + pull only.

(We don't use SSH key, since we don't plan to push from the VM to github)

Where to paste:

Deploy key: GitHub repo [BCFMS-project-management](https://github.com/yeroslaviz/BCFMS-project-management) -> Settings -> Deploy keys -> Add deploy key.

## Confirm GitHub access and pull latest code.

```bash
ssh -T git@github.com
git ls-remote git@github.com:example-org/example-repo.git
```

## Clone repository

```bash
cd /home/<user>/
git clone git@github.com:<user>/BCFMS-project-management.git
cd /home/<user>/BCFMS-project-management
git pull --ff-only origin main
```

## Enable push from the VM

If the VM currently uses a deploy key, like we do on this VM, `push` will be denied. To allow `push`, we have to add an SSH key attached to a GitHub user that has write permission.

Set Git identity on the VM:

```bash
git config --global user.name "Example User"
git config --global user.email "example.user@example.org"
git config --global pull.rebase false
```
Optional write test:

```bash
cd /home/<user>/BCFMS-project-management
git checkout -b test/vm-write-check
git push -u origin test/vm-write-check
```

If push succeeds, delete the test branch:

```bash
git checkout main
git branch -D test/vm-write-check
git push origin --delete test/vm-write-check
```
